meta.setting: Snow edges, stock country, Klamath mountain ridges, and rumor-heavy road camps
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with rumor, livestock fear, disputed sign, hunting pressure, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Gray Wolf
research_grounding.use: Gray wolves as a native species likely extirpated from California in the 1920s, making 1899 wolf sign rare, disputed, and plausible at the edge.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Deer as prey and track context.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Elk
research_grounding.use: Elk and herd-scale landscape context for northern/wider frontier scenes.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Wolf Rumors cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented snow-track note, stock warning, or camp rumor generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Old Pack Talk
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing rare wolf sign, disputed stories, stock fear, and memory of a native predator near the edge of disappearance
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four grounded signs or rumor patterns
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, warning, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate wolves as rare, contested, and rumor-heavy for 1899, not common enemies.
Tie future entries to disputed tracks, distant howls, livestock panic, old hunter claims, mistaken dog sign, snow reading, dead calves, bounty talk, and moral choices around extermination.
Make uncertainty playable: the page should ask what people do before they know.
Use the rumor itself as a social animal: it changes prices, patrols, blame, and who gets permission to cross land.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The best wolf page is mostly absence: a howl, a missing calf, and three men lying for different reasons.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A rumor can travel farther than a pack and bite nearly as hard.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western animal-sign and rumor language only.
Keep wolves rare, naturalistic, disputed, and historically plausible for 1899.
The generated page must include image path images/wolf-rumors.jpg.
No finished page prose, final entries, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred-invented, or fake-tribal framing
do not make wolves common monsters, pets, boss fights, mascots, or moral symbols
do not use modern wildlife gear, vehicles, plastic props, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about rare wolf sign, disputed rumor, livestock fear, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
